commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a documented `openclaw sessions compact <key>` command, rejects CLI `/compact` messages with exit 1, forwards selected session options, and adds CLI/docs/tests for those paths.

PR surface: Source +191, Tests +157, Docs +54. Total +402 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main has a `sessions.compact` Gateway method but no first-class `sessions compact` CLI command, and CLI `/compact` is only guarded after a gateway timeout rather than rejected up front.
commands.review_followup: - The remaining blockers are narrow mechanical fixes in CLI formatting and option validation, with clear files and focused tests.

target.title: feat(cli): add `openclaw sessions compact` and fail loudly on CLI `/compact` (fixes #90640)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90640.

`sessions.compact` was reachable only as an internal Gateway RPC: there was no
CLI command and no docs, and `openclaw agent --message '/compact'` silently
no-opped with exit 0 (the slash-command handler rejects CLI-originated senders,
so the message fell through to an ordinary agent turn that compacted nothing).

This PR closes both gaps:

- Adds `openclaw sessions compact <key>` as a first-class command wrapping the
  existing `sessions.compact` RPC. It exits non-zero whenever the gateway
  reports a failed compaction — both a transport error and an `ok:false`
  payload — so automation never mistakes a silent no-op for success.
- Makes `openclaw agent --message '/compact'` fail loudly: it prints a redirect
  to `openclaw sessions compact <key>` and exits 1 instead of silently exiting
  0. The shared chat-side `/compact` handler is left untouched, so chat
  behaviour and message delivery are unchanged.
- Inherits the parent `sessions` options for `compact`, matching its sibling
  subcommands (`list`/`cleanup`/`info`/…). Previously a parent-level
  `--agent`/`--json` was silently dropped, so `openclaw sessions --agent work
  compact <key>` compacted the **default** agent's session instead of the
  `work` agent's — a wrong-target session-state mutation. The compact-level
  option still takes precedence over the inherited parent option.
- Strictly validates `--max-lines` and `--timeout` (positive integers only).
- Documents the command and the `sessions.compact` RPC in docs/cli/sessions.md.

## Why this is safe (vs. touching the shared handler)

The fix is confined to the CLI `--message` dispatch path and the CLI command
registration. It does **not** modify the shared `commands-compact.ts` handler
that powers chat `/compact`, so there is no compatibility or message-delivery
blast radius — only the CLI path that was already broken changes behaviour.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `openclaw sessions compact <key>` must drive
  a real compaction end-to-end through the new CLI command body (not just the
  raw RPC), respecting a parent-level `--agent`; and
  `openclaw agent --message '/compact'` must fail loudly with a non-zero exit
  instead of silently exiting 0 without compacting anything.

- **Real environment tested**: Both proofs drive the **production CLI command
  functions** (`sessionsCompactCommand` and `agentCliCommand`) against a **real
  in-process OpenClaw Gateway server** over a live WebSocket
  (`ws://127.0.0.1:<port>`), with a temporary on-disk session transcript seeded
  with 500 JSONL lines. Terminal output is captured from each command's own
  `runtime.log` / `runtime.error`, and the transcript files are read back from
  disk.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: (1) Started the real
  Gateway, seeded `agent:main:main` with a 500-line on-disk transcript, then
  invoked the production `sessions compact` command function with
  `key: "agent:main:main"`, `maxLines: 50`, and the gateway `url`/`token` —
  exercising the full CLI command body → callGateway → gateway RPC →
  filesystem path. (2) Invoked the production `agent` command function with
  `--message '/compact'`.

- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output captured from the real command
  functions:

```text
PROOF A — openclaw sessions compact <key>
  url: ws://127.0.0.1:30000
  command stdout (runtime.log):
    Compacted session agent:main:main (kept 50 lines).
    Archived transcript: <tmp>/sess-main.jsonl.bak.2026-06-08T11-08-40.217Z
  command stderr (runtime.error): (none)
  exit code: 0 (process.exit not called = success)
  transcript lines BEFORE compact (on disk): 500
  transcript lines AFTER  compact (on disk): 50
  .bak archive lines (original preserved):   500

PROOF B — openclaw agent --message '/compact'
  command stderr (runtime.error):
    Slash commands cannot be executed via --message from the CLI.
    Use: openclaw sessions compact <key>
  exit code: 1
```

- **Observed result after fix**: Proof A — the production `sessions compact`
  command printed the human-facing success line
  (`Compacted session agent:main:main (kept 50 lines).`) and the archived
  `.bak` path, reduced the active transcript from 500 → 50 lines on disk, and
  preserved the original 500 lines verbatim in the `.bak` archive — all through
  the new CLI command body, not the raw RPC. Proof B — `agent --message
  '/compact'` emitted the redirect message on stderr and exited 1.

- **What was not tested**: No GUI or screenshot flow; this proof targets the
  real CLI command functions, Gateway, and session-file behaviour directly.

## Tests

- `src/commands/sessions-compact.test.ts` — 5 unit tests: success token delta,
  `ok:false` → exit 1, RPC throw → exit 1, JSON-mode `ok:false` → payload +
  exit 1, params forwarding (`agentId`/`maxLines`).
- `src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts` — parametrized cases proving
  `/compact`, `/compact <text>`, `/compact:<text>`, `/COMPACT`, and
  `  /Compact  ` are all rejected with exit 1 before any gateway or embedded
  turn, while `/compacting …` is NOT mistaken for a control command.
- `src/cli/program/register.status-health-sessions.test.ts` — regression
  coverage for parent-option inheritance: a parent `sessions --agent`/`--json`
  is forwarded to `compact`, and a compact-level `--agent` overrides the parent
  (29 tests pass).
target.author: alix-007

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
